answer:
Authentication for Salesforce Commerce Cloud APIs depends on whether you are accessing Admin or Shopper endpoints.

Admin APIs are secured via Account Manager [1][2]. You request an access token from the oauth2 resource using a client_credentials grant type, providing your client ID and secret in the request [1].

```bash
curl "https://account.demandware.com/dwsso/oauth2/access_token" \
--request 'POST' \
--user "client_id:client_secret" \
--header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
--data "grant_type=client_credentials" \
--data-urlencode "scope=SALESFORCE_COMMERCE_API:realm_instance_id [scopes]"
```

Shopper APIs use the Shopper Login and API Access Service (SLAS) [2][3]. Authorization flows vary by client type and user authentication method (e.g., authorization_code_pkce for public clients or client_credentials for private systems) [3].
